items.headline: City launches business license amnesty program for local shops
items.summary: Cupertino is offering an amnesty program to help unlicensed businesses at shopping centers get into compliance. The city is also running a 'Shop Local' campaign and new economic incentive programs to support retail and small businesses.

items.headline: New mixed-use housing project approved at East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View City Council approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential project at 490 East Middlefield Road. This project will add new housing to the area and was voted on for a second reading on March 24.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for California's Prohousing Incentive Program Round 4, which provides state funding to support housing development. This funding could help accelerate new housing projects in Mountain View.

items.headline: City seeking public input on $HUD housing funds for next year
items.summary: Santa Clara is opening a public comment period on how to spend federal HUD grant money for housing and community development in fiscal year 2026/27. This is your chance to weigh in on housing priorities before the city finalizes its spending plan.

items.headline: City holding community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale is hosting a public meeting on Monday, March 23 at 5:30 p.m. to discuss safe parking, likely a program addressing overnight parking for people experiencing homelessness or housing insecurity. This appears to be a new or expanded initiative worth learning about if you care about homelessness policy in the South Bay.

items.headline: Station Area Task Force reviewing land use and design for transit zone
items.summary: A city task force is reviewing updated land use plans and design guidelines for the area around Santa Clara Station. This could shape what gets built and how it looks near the transit hub.
